testCases.query: I need to build an image captioning pipeline that processes user-uploaded photos and returns descriptions. Which vision model should I use and how do I send base64 images?
expectedBehavior: Claude references the vision-image-analysis and vision-models rules
Recommends a vision model based on accuracy/cost tradeoffs (e.g., Claude Opus for high accuracy, Gemini Flash for cost efficiency)
Shows base64 encoding pattern with max_tokens set and image resizing guidance
Warns about not sending oversized images (>2048px)

testCases.query: I want to generate a 6-scene product video with consistent character identity across all clips using Kling 3.0
expectedBehavior: Claude references video-multi-shot and video-generation-patterns rules
Explains Character Elements 3.0 for identity binding across scenes
Covers async task polling pattern (not synchronous calls)
Mentions storyboarding workflow for multi-shot consistency

testCases.query: Help me set up a REST API with Express.js and connect it to a PostgreSQL database
expectedBehavior: Claude does NOT invoke the multimodal-llm skill
Uses standard backend development knowledge instead
No references to vision, audio, video generation, or multimodal patterns

testCases.query: I'm building a real-time voice assistant and considering chaining separate STT, LLM, and TTS steps. Which audio model architecture should I use for the lowest latency?
expectedBehavior: Claude recommends native speech-to-speech instead of chaining STT plus LLM plus TTS
Claude compares Grok Voice Agent, Gemini Live, and OpenAI Realtime with latency tradeoffs
Claude warns that a three-step pipeline adds two to five times more latency than native models
Claude recommends under one second time-to-first-audio as the quality threshold for phone agents

testCases.query: I need to transcribe a two-hour meeting recording with speaker labels and timestamps. Which speech-to-text model handles long audio best and provides diarization?
expectedBehavior: Claude recommends Gemini 2.5 Pro for long-form audio handling up to 9.5 hours natively
Claude warns against using deprecated Whisper-1 and recommends GPT-4o-Transcribe instead
Claude suggests AssemblyAI for the richest metadata including sentiment and entity detection
Claude advises specifying the language when known to improve transcription accuracy significantly

testCases.query: I want to generate natural-sounding speech from text with a specific voice and expressive style. How do I configure voice selection with Gemini TTS?
expectedBehavior: Claude configures Gemini TTS with explicit voice selection using PrebuiltVoiceConfig
Claude sets response_mime_type to audio/mp3 in the GenerationConfig for audio output
Claude mentions that Gemini offers 30 HD voices across 24 languages for selection
Claude explains that Grok supports inline auditory cues like whisper, sigh, and pause

testCases.query: I need to extract data from a 50-page PDF report with charts and tables. How do I process it with a vision model without hitting context limits?
expectedBehavior: Claude uses page ranges to process the PDF incrementally with max 20 pages per request
Claude never sends the entire large PDF at once to avoid timeouts and context overflow
Claude uses detail high for documents with small text, tables, or charts
Claude recommends Gemini 2.5 Pro for longest document context with over one million tokens

testCases.query: I'm sending images to GPT for analysis but the model seems to ignore them. How do I properly encode and structure base64 images in the API request?
expectedBehavior: Claude uses structured content array with image_url type instead of string content
Claude always sets max_tokens on vision requests to prevent response truncation
Claude shows the correct base64 data URI format with proper mime type prefix
Claude warns that each provider has a different content structure and formats must not be mixed

testCases.query: I need to classify thousands of product images as approved or rejected. Which vision model is most cost-effective for simple yes/no classification tasks?
expectedBehavior: Claude recommends a cheaper model with detail low setting at 65 tokens per image
Claude warns against using expensive models with high detail for simple classification tasks
Claude provides the model comparison table with context, strengths, and max images per request
Claude notes that vision models cannot identify specific people due to privacy restrictions

testCases.query: I need to generate short product showcase videos at scale for social media. Which video generation model offers the best balance of cost, speed, and character consistency?
expectedBehavior: Claude recommends Kling 3.0 Standard for high-volume social content at the lowest cost per video
Claude warns that Sora 2 is expensive and slow for volume work at over one dollar per video
Claude compares models by duration, resolution, native audio, and character consistency capabilities
Claude notes that video generation is always async and requires polling or callback patterns

testCases.query: I'm trying to generate videos with the Kling API but my requests keep timing out. How do I properly submit a video generation task and retrieve the result?
expectedBehavior: Claude implements async task submission followed by status polling instead of synchronous calls
Claude uses exponential backoff on polling and does not hammer the API every second
Claude stores the task_id persistently so polling can resume after a process crash
Claude recommends fal.ai or AI SDK abstractions over raw REST for new projects

testCases.query: I need to create a three-scene video where the same character appears in each scene with consistent identity. How do I use Kling multi-shot with character elements?
expectedBehavior: Claude uses Kling 3.0 multi-shot with character elements for identity-consistent scenes
Claude provides up to three reference images including a required frontal face view
Claude references characters as @Element1 in per-shot prompts for character binding
Claude notes that multi-shot requires mode pro and only works with Kling 3.0 or later
